channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Prosecutors to recommend resentencing Erik and Lyle Menendez in 1989 killings of their parents
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Prosecutors will recommend Erik and Lyle Menendez be resentenced for the 1989 killings of their parents in the family’s Beverly Hills home, providing the brothers with a chance at freedom after 34 years behind bars.
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO and JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: EPA imposes stricter standards to protect children from exposure to lead paint
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Two weeks after setting a nationwide deadline for removal of lead pipes, the Biden administration is imposing strict new limits on dust from lead-based paint in older homes and child-care facilities.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: County judge strikes down Ohio abortion ban, citing voter-approved reproductive rights amendment
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — The most far-reaching of Ohio’s laws restricting abortion was struck down on Thursday by a county judge who said last year’s voter-approved amendment enshrining reproductive rights renders the so-called heartbeat law unconstitutional.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A cub of famous Wyoming grizzly No. 399 has been unseen since his mom's death but odds look good
item.description: The death of a famous grizzly bear on a highway in western Wyoming has made an orphan of its cub, leading many to wonder about his fate.
item.dc:creator: Mead Gruver, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: By moving to podcasts, Harris and Trump are turning away from legacy media to spread their messages
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Among the legacy news outlets that have come up empty in their efforts to interview Kamala Harris and Donald Trump during the general election campaign: NPR, The New York Times, PBS and The Washington Post.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: President Biden to apologize for 150-year Indian boarding school policy
item.description: NORMAN, Okla. (AP) — President Joe Biden said he will formally apologize on Friday for the country’s role in forcing Indigenous children into boarding schools, where many were physically, emotionally, and sexually abused and nearly 1,000 died.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Biden to apologize for policy sending Native American children to forced-assimilation boarding schools
item.description: President Biden plans to issue an official apology for a U.S. policy that for 150 years sent Native American children to boarding schools where they were stripped of their culture and language.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ex-officer on trial in shooting death of Andre Hill thought his life was in danger, attorney says
item.description: A white former Ohio police officer mistakenly shot and killed Andre Hill as the Black man was emerging from a garage nearly four years ago because he saw a flash of light from keys in Hill’s hand and thought he was about to be shot, the officer’s attorney said Thursday during opening statements of h
item.dc:creator: JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: DAY-O! Airbnb wants you to stay at the Deetz house this Halloween
item.description: Halloween is just around the corner, and Airbnb wants you to stay at the famous house from Tim Burton's dark fantasy film, Beetlejuice!
item.dc:creator: Garrett Phillips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: World on pace for significantly more warming without immediate climate action, report warns
item.description: The world is on a path to get 1.8 degrees Celsius (3.2 Fahrenheit) warmer than it is now, but could trim half a degree of that projected future heating if countries do everything they promise to fight climate change, a United Nations report said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Kentucky lawmaker dies weeks after lawnmower plunges into swimming pool
item.description: Kentucky state Sen. Johnnie Turner (R) died Tuesday from injuries he suffered after being thrown into an empty swimming pool while riding his lawnmower just over a month ago.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: McDonald's and Boar's Head outbreaks may have you worried. Experts say the food supply is safe
item.description: Deadly food poisoning outbreaks tied to McDonald's and Boar's Head may have left Americans wondering whether there are new risks in the U.S. food supply.
item.dc:creator: Jonel Aleccia, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Lifelong 'rivals' recall love and admiration for pitching legend Fernando Valenzuela
item.description: "Nobody will ever do what he did, we lost a living legend, he was me, he was you, he was our brothers, sisters, cousins, he was us," said Hernandez. "He represented us, he looked like us, talked like us, acted like us, and the loss is for every single one of us.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: Georgia's Gullah-Geechee community seeks path forward after deadly dock collapse
item.description: SAVANNAH, Ga. (AP) — The partial collapse of a ferry dock that claimed the lives of seven people attending a cultural festival has left a historic Black community in Georgia struggling with how to move forward.
item.dc:creator: TERRY TANG and RUSS BYNUM, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Louisiana governor forces New Orleans homeless encampment to move ahead of Taylor Swift shows
item.description: Ahead of Taylor Swift shows in New Orleans this weekend, Louisiana Gov. Jeff Landry authorized the relocation of a homeless encampment from the popular tourist area.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Boots for dogs become 'Croctober' smash; sell out fast
item.description: Footwear company Crocs developed a special edition boot for dogs in celebration of "Croctober" and they were a big hit. The shoe brand releases a fan-inspired product each October and this year it was all about man's best friend, KTLA sister station WKRG reported earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, KTLA 5

item.title: DNC hits Trump over reported praise of Hitler's generals in mobile billboard
item.description: The Democratic National Committee (DNC) is deploying a mobile billboard ahead of former President Donald Trump’s rally in Las Vegas on Thursday, hitting him over his past reported praise of Adolf Hitler’s generals.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, Fox 5

item.title: Suspect in shootings of Democratic office in Arizona had over 120 guns in his home, officials find
item.description: TEMPE, Ariz. (AP) — An Arizona prosecutor said the man arrested in the shooting of a Democratic National Committee office in suburban Phoenix had more than 120 guns and over 250,000 rounds of ammunition in his home, leading law enforcement to believe he may have been planning a mass casualty event.
item.dc:creator: ANITA SNOW and WALTER BERRY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: No end for Boeing labor strike as workers reject latest contract proposal
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — Boeing factory workers have voted against the company’s latest contract offer and will remain on the picket lines six weeks into a strike that has stopped production of the aerospace giant’s bestselling jetliners.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Letter at center of Menendez brothers' bid for freedom called into question
item.description: Kitty Menendez's brother questions letter at heart of Lyle and Erik Menendez's appeal for freedom despite life without parole for shotgun murder of their parents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump assassination attempt: Secret Service knew airspace protection would end with former president onstage
item.description: Additional lapses in public airspace restrictions on the day of former President Trump's July 13 rally continues to raise questions and concerns about security.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Grieving families of seniors killed when a dock collapsed say the tragedy was preventable
item.description: JACKSONVILLE, Fla. (AP) — Regina Brinson heard a crack before the metal walkway gave way beneath her feet, plunging her into the water beneath the state-operated ferry dock on Georgia's Sapelo Island.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US charges Iran Revolutionary Guard official in alleged plot to kill a human rights activist in NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Federal prosecutors on Tuesday charged a senior Iranian military official and three others with links to that country's government with plotting to kill an Iranian American author on U.S. soil — a development lauded by the alleged target. “This is a beautiful day for me.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge orders Giuliani to hand over assets to Georgia election workers
item.description: Rudy Giuliani must turn over much of his property — his apartment in New York, the city that made him mayor, and other prized possessions — to two Georgia election workers who have sought for months to collect their massive defamation judgment, a federal judge ruled Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: LIST: Waffles, pancakes recall expanded over potential listeria contamination
item.description: The recalled products were sold under multiple brand names across the entire country, the company says.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Asheville annual holiday tradition canceled due to Helene
item.description: ASHEVILLE, N.C. (WSPA) - An annual national holiday tradition that has taken place for over three decades in Asheville has been canceled due to the aftermath of Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Nikolette Miller, Fox 5

item.title: 19-year-old woman found dead in oven in Canada Walmart
item.description: A Canadian Walmart store was closed for a third day Tuesday as police investigated the death of a woman whose body was found inside an oven.
item.dc:creator: Wil Day, Fox 5

item.title: Insurance company refuses to pay 82-year-old Las Vegas woman's claim after her home burned down
item.description: Two weeks before the fire, the insurance company canceled the woman's policy, accusing her of running a motorcycle repair shop out of her home — something the 82-year-old woman denies.
item.dc:creator: Kyle J. Paine, Fox 5

item.title: Mike Jeffries, former Abercrombie & Fitch CEO, arrested on federal sex trafficking charges
item.description: Former Abercrombie & Fitch CEO Michael Jeffries and two other men have been arrested on sex trafficking and interstate prostitution charges, a spokesperson for federal prosecutors said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: On Lake Erie, getting rid of problem algae starts with giving it less food
item.description: OREGON, Ohio (AP) — On a warm late-summer evening, a small speedboat motored across a pea-green stretch of Lake Erie past a beach where a child sat splashing and a pair of newlyweds waded for a portrait photographer.
item.dc:creator: JOSHUA A. BICKEL and PATRICK AFTOORA-ORSAGOS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New campus protest rules spur an outcry from college faculty
item.description: Dissent is thriving this fall at American colleges, and not just among student activists. With student protests limited by new restrictions, faculty have taken up the cause. To faculty, new protest rules threaten freedom of speech — and the freedom to think, both central to university life.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge hears arguments to block Louisiana's Ten Commandments displays in schools
item.description: BATON ROUGE, La. (AP) — A federal judge heard arguments at a hearing Monday on whether he should temporarily block a new Louisiana law that requires the Ten Commandments to be displayed in every public school classroom by Jan. 1.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: PHOTOS: White House public tour gets makeover with 'reimagined' visitor experience
item.description: The White House public tour is getting an extreme makeover, with new digital exhibits, an expanded route and more access to rooms poised to be part of the "reimagined" experience for visitors.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: McDonald's says it doesn't endorse political candidates after Trump visit
item.description: McDonald's is emphasizing its political neutrality after former President Donald Trump spent some time behind the counter at one of its Pennsylvania locations.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, Fox 5

item.title: WATCH: Footage appears to show moment helicopter crash topples radio tower in Houston
item.description: A surveillance camera mounted outside of a Houston photo studio appears to have captured the moment a helicopter crash toppled a radio tower on Sunday, killing four people.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4

item.title: Central Park Five sue Donald Trump for jogger case remarks made at presidential debate
item.description: With Election Day two weeks away, the group accused the former president of making “false and defamatory statements.
item.dc:creator: TERRY TANG, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Utah court allows Australian student to play high school football after lawsuit filed
item.description: An international student who filed a discrimination lawsuit earlier this month has been allowed to play on his high school's football team.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, WGN 9
